Character.Description: A highly competent, focused woman coder and systems thinker

Face.Expression: Focused, slightly amused — she's playtesting, not just playing
Face.Makeup: Bold, pinks or purples, with a touch of shimmer

Hair.Style: Flowing wavy curls, partly catching the screen glow from above

Base.Name: Dark blazer
Base.Description: Professional dark blazer over tech-casual attire, sleeves pushed up to the elbows ready to work the joystick and buttons

Style.Rendering: High-detail digital painting, photorealistic, concept art quality

Sources: Arcade CRT screen glow as primary key light
Subtle rim light along her shoulders and hair
Lighting.Shadows: Sharp, emphasizing the silhouette of her shoulders and head against the bright CRT

ColorPalette.Treatment: High saturation, strong contrast between warm marquee light and cool CRT phosphor wash

Effects: Atmospheric haze in the arcade hall
CRT scanlines visible on the screen
Faint dust motes in the screen-glow beam

Situation.Name: Playtesting Space Invaders on the Cabinet
Situation.Setting: A dimly lit retro arcade hall, rows of cabinets receding into atmospheric haze. The hero cabinet is a tall vertical 1978-era Space Invaders machine — black cabinet with the iconic blue-and-orange side art, illuminated marquee at the top reading 'SPACE INVADERS', a sloped control panel with the authentic 1978 Taito layout — a single short joystick with a white ball top on the left and a single round red FIRE button on the right, nothing else, and a CRT monitor showing an active mid-game state
Situation.Camera: Over-the-shoulder shot from behind and slightly above her right shoulder. Her silhouette occupies the left third of the frame; the CRT screen is the visual focal point, clearly readable, occupying the right two-thirds of the frame. Shallow depth of field — she and the cabinet are crisp, the arcade hall behind blurs into bokeh

CharacterPose.Expression: Focused concentration with a slight amused half-smile — the look of someone evaluating an artefact, not just enjoying it
CharacterPose.BodyPosition: Standing at the cabinet, weight slightly forward, leaning in toward the CRT, shoulders squared to the screen
CharacterPose.Gesture: Left hand holding the white LEFT/RIGHT joystick ball (visible at the lower edge of the control panel), right hand poised over the round red FIRE button — historically accurate 1978 Taito control layout

Character.Name: Dark blazer with sleeves pushed up
Character.Description: Sleeves rolled to mid-forearm, ready for the controls

Article.Name: Authentic 1978 Taito Space Invaders upright cabinet
Article.Description: Match the provided reference image exactly in every detail. Tall vertical upright cabinet with cobalt-blue side panels covered in the iconic black silhouetted mutant/monster artwork and orange-and-yellow lunar-landscape illustrations along the bottom edge. White 'SPACE INVADERS' wordmark in the angular 1978 logotype displayed on the side panel. Backlit marquee at the top featuring stars, an astronaut figure, and 'SPACE INVADERS' branding against a dark space background. CRT monitor recessed behind a black bezel showing the playfield. Below the CRT bezel, a recessed control panel with the historically authentic 1978 Taito Space Invaders control layout: exactly one short joystick with a white ball top on the left side of the panel for left/right movement, and exactly one round red FIRE button on the right side. No other controls on the player panel — no extra buttons, no second joystick, no trackball. Below the control panel, a large chrome/silver embossed coin-door panel with cash-box bezel dominates the lower-front face of the cabinet — this is the most visually distinctive front feature after the marquee. Wood-grain top edge trim. Stout pedestal base. Every element must match the reference image; do not invent controls that are not in the reference.
Article.Application: The hero cabinet, occupying the right two-thirds of the frame, captured in three-quarter view so both the front (control panel, CRT, chrome coin door) and the side panel (with the SPACE INVADERS wordmark and monster art) are visible
Article.SymbolicMeaning: The artefact being evaluated — the deliverable the bake-off was measured against

Article.Name: CRT screen content
Article.Description: Visible through the cabinet bezel: vertical black playfield showing the classic 5-row alien formation (5 rows of 11 invaders) mid-march, four green shield bunkers below, a small white player cannon at the bottom firing a single white bullet upward. Green and white phosphor colors only — period-correct. Score readout '0420' at the top, '03' lives indicator, 'HI-SCORE 9990'. Visible CRT scanlines and slight curvature distortion at the edges of the tube
Article.Application: Inside the cabinet bezel, glowing brightly enough to be the dominant light source on her face and shoulder
Article.SymbolicMeaning: Mid-game state of the deliverable under evaluation

Environment.Name: Row of background arcade cabinets
Environment.Description: Other vintage cabinets receding into the haze, marquees glowing in different neon colors (magenta, cyan, lime green), suggesting a busy arcade hall

Environment.Name: Dark patterned carpet floor
Environment.Description: Classic arcade-style geometric patterned carpet, barely visible in the dim light

Environment.Name: Hanging neon sign in distance
Environment.Description: A faint magenta or cyan tube-sign far in the background, contributing atmospheric color
Environment.Application: Far background, very out of focus

KeyDetails: The CRT screen content must be unmistakably authentic Space Invaders — the alien formation, the bunkers, the player cannon must all be legible
Her silhouette is partly backlit by the marquee above and side-lit by the CRT — creating a clean cinematic profile of her face and shoulder
Her amused-focused expression sells the framing: this is a playtest, not nostalgia
Over-the-shoulder framing — camera is clearly behind her, we see the back/side of her head and shoulder, never her full face
Situation.LightingFocus: CRT phosphor green/white as the primary key light on her face, side and hair; warm amber marquee light from above provides rim lighting on the top of her shoulders and the crown of her hair; the rest of the arcade hall fades into cool blue-violet ambient haze

ArticleContext.Title: Seven Coding Agents, One Space Invaders Clone

CompositionNotes.Framing: Over-the-shoulder wide shot. Character silhouette left third, CRT screen right two-thirds
CompositionNotes.SubjectPlacement: Character clearly behind/foreground left, cabinet and screen as visual focal point right
CompositionNotes.DepthOfField: Shallow — character and cabinet crisp, arcade hall in bokeh
CompositionNotes.TextSafeArea: Upper-left and lower-left have room for text overlay if needed
